seo_title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via…
meta_description: A new framework, CORDON-MAS, reduces attack success rate by 92.4% in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
body_html: Researchers have developed a new framework called CORDON-MAS to defend retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) models against knowledge poisoning attacks, which involve manipulating generated outputs with adversarially optimized documents. The existing defenses assume that detecting poisoned evidence prevents harm, but the new framework shows that this assumption is incorrect and proposes an alternative approach by separating evidence extraction, cross-source audit, and answer synthesis into agents with asymmetric memory privileges. This results in a significant reduction of attack success rate by 92.4% relative to undefended RAG models across five benchmark datasets.

source.title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via Information-Flow Control
